tests.test_name: pytest - PO - single test, core output structure
tests.description: Verifies that uploading a single pytest file and generating documentation produces valid, complete output with at least one named test case.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system responds visibly after processing the uploaded test file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: No placeholder or missing data exists in the generated documentation
At least one test case is captured from the uploaded file

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each documented test case is identifiable by name

tests.test_name: pytest - PO - parametrize expands one test per row
tests.description: Verifies that a data-driven pytest file with parametrize is expanded so that each parameter combination appears as its own separate test case in the Product Owner output.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system responds visibly after processing the data-driven test file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: No placeholder or missing data exists in the generated documentation
Each parameter row in the data-driven test is expanded into its own test entry

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each expanded test case is individually identifiable by name

tests.test_name: pytest - QAM - single test, coverage matrix populated
tests.description: Verifies that uploading a single pytest file in QA Manager mode produces valid documentation that includes a populated test coverage matrix for the first test.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system responds visibly after processing the uploaded test file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: No placeholder or missing data exists in the generated documentation
At least one test case is captured from the uploaded file

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each documented test case is identifiable by name

test_coverage.verification_step: First test has a non-empty test_coverage_matrix array
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The QA Manager view includes a coverage matrix with at least one entry for the test

tests.description: Verifies that a data-driven pytest file in QA Manager mode produces a single collapsed test entry whose coverage matrix rows match the number of parameter combinations and follow the correct schema.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system responds visibly after processing the data-driven test file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: No placeholder or missing data exists in the generated documentation
All parameter combinations are collapsed into a single test entry rather than expanded

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The collapsed test case is identifiable by name

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix row count matches the examples
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each parameter combination is represented as its own row within the coverage matrix

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix rows use the correct schema for generic
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each coverage matrix row conforms to the expected data structure for generic test types
